agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. Enforces accrual-based balance integrity, advance notice requirements, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover limits.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued, unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which leave days are earned. Employees accrue 1.5 days per month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to consume accrued leave days for a specified date range. Must be approved by a manager before leave is confirmed."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days of the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, bypassing the standard advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays, used to calculate the 3-day advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave balance is transferred to the next year, capped at 10 days. Days above the cap are forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days that exceed the 10-day carryover cap at year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical document required to validate retroactive medical leave submissions."},{"term":"Approval","definition":"A manager's decision to grant a leave request, at which point the leave balance is consumed."},{"term":"Rejection","definition":"A manager's decision to deny a leave request. No leave balance is consumed."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per calendar month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The accrual rate of 1.5 days/month is a company policy that HR leadership could adjust (e.g., to 2.0 days for senior employees). It is not a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"After completing one full month of employment, the employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance must never go below zero. An employee cannot consume more leave than they have accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative leave balance means the system has granted time off that was never earned — this is a data integrity violation. The employee would be paid for days they have no entitlement to.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leaveBalance >= 0. Any operation that would reduce balance below zero must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Standard (non-medical) leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the first day of requested leave.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-working-day notice period is an administrative policy that management could relax or tighten. Violating it does not corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before the leave start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a valid doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day retroactive window and documentation requirement are policy decisions that could be adjusted without corrupting data.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted more than 5 calendar days after the absence date is rejected. A medical leave request submitted within 5 days but without a doctor's note attached is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's manager may approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization integrity — if a non-manager can approve leave, the approval chain is broken and the system's trust model is violated.","testable_assertion":"An approve or reject action on a leave request succeeds only when performed by the employee's designated manager."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave requests do not reduce the employee's leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Consuming balance for rejected leave is a data corruption scenario — the employee loses days for time they were told they cannot take off.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to 'rejected' status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from the pre-request value."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request's date range must not extend beyond the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Leave beyond a contract end date is logically impossible — the employee will not be employed. Allowing it creates phantom obligations and payroll errors.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where any requested day falls on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave balance above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carryover cap is a company policy that could be changed by HR leadership (e.g., raised to 15 days during COVID). Not a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, if an employee's prior-year ending balance was N days, the new-year opening balance is min(N, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked a full calendar month","when":"The monthly accrual process runs","then":"The employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and the balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The leave start date is fewer than 3 working days from the submission date","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient notice error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 4 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted for manager review despite being retroactive"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 6 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because the 5-day retroactive window has expired"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A medical leave request is submitted within 5 days of absence","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected with a missing documentation error"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A leave request is in 'pending' status","when":"The employee's manager approves it","then":"The request status becomes 'approved' and the employee's leave balance is reduced by the number of requested days"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A leave request is in 'pending' status","when":"The employee's manager rejects it","then":"The request status becomes 'rejected' and the employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days at December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"The employee's opening balance on January 1 is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days at December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"The employee's opening balance on January 1 is 8 days with no forfeiture"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A leave request is in 'pending' status","when":"A user who is not the employee's manager attempts to approve or reject it","then":"The action is denied with an authorization error"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee starts mid-month (e.g., hired on the 15th)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define partial-month accrual. Business must clarify: does '1 month worked' mean calendar month or 30 days? Pro-rata for partial months, or accrual only after a full month?"},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted exactly 3 working days in advance","expected_behavior":"The 'at least 3 working days' rule means exactly 3 working days should be accepted. The boundary is inclusive."},{"scenario":"Medical leave retroactive submission on exactly day 5","expected_behavior":"'Within 5 days' should include day 5 (inclusive boundary). Request should be accepted."},{"scenario":"Leave request spans weekends or holidays","expected_behavior":"Spec does not clarify whether weekends/holidays within a leave range consume leave balance. Business must define whether leave days are counted as calendar days or working days."},{"scenario":"Contract end date falls within an already-approved leave period","expected_behavior":"If a contract end date is moved earlier after leave was already approved, the system must handle the conflict — likely auto-canceling leave days that now fall beyond the new end date."},{"scenario":"Manager approves leave but employee's balance was consumed by another concurrent approval","expected_behavior":"A race condition: two pending requests each individually fit the balance but together exceed it. The second approval must re-validate the balance at approval time, not submission time."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover with pending leave requests that span the year boundary","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address this. If an employee has 14 days balance and 5 days approved leave starting Dec 29, does carryover apply to 14 or 9? Must clarify whether approved-but-not-yet-taken leave is deducted before carryover."},{"scenario":"Employee has zero months worked (just started)","expected_behavior":"Balance should be 0. Any leave request should be rejected for insufficient balance."},{"scenario":"Medical leave for a future date with a doctor's note","expected_behavior":"Medical leave exception only permits retroactive submission. A future-dated medical leave must follow standard 3-working-day advance notice rules."},{"scenario":"Leave request for exactly the contract end date itself","expected_behavior":"Spec says leave cannot span 'across' the end date. Need clarification: is the contract end date the last working day, or the first day the employee is no longer employed? Leave on the last working day should likely be allowed."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or balance tracking directly impacts payroll — overpaid leave creates unrecoverable costs. Forfeiture errors create either unearned liability on the books or employee disputes. For a 500-person company at avg salary, a 1-day accrual error compounds to significant unplanned cost.","operational":"Leave approval workflow directly affects team capacity planning. If advance notice rules are not enforced, managers lose ability to plan coverage. Carryover errors at year-end create HR administrative burden and potential legal disputes.","user":"Employees expect transparent, fair leave tracking. Rejections must have clear reasons. Retroactive medical leave is a high-sensitivity flow — sick employees should not face unnecessary friction. Poor UX here drives HR ticket volume.","risk":"Negative balance is a compliance and audit risk. Failure to enforce contract end date boundaries could create legal exposure if leave is granted post-termination. Carryover forfeiture without prior notification to employees may violate labor regulations in some jurisdictions.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct — their leave entitlement, balance visibility, and request experience. Errors erode trust in the HR system."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Approval workflow efficiency and team capacity planning. False approvals or missed rules create management overhead."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Policy enforcement, audit trail integrity, dispute resolution. They bear the cost of every system error."},{"group":"Payroll","impact":"Leave balance feeds into payroll calculations. Incorrect balances cascade into pay errors."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave liability is a balance sheet item. Incorrect carryover or accrual inflates or understates liabilities."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is reasonably clear on core rules but leaves several operational details ambiguous: partial-month accrual, working-day vs calendar-day leave counting, carryover interaction with pending/approved leave, and whether contract end date is inclusive or exclusive. No contradictions detected, but multiple edge cases require business clarification before implementation can be fully correct.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate of 1.5 days per month","Rejected leave does not consume balance","3-working-day advance notice for standard leave","Year-end carryover cap of 10 days"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial-month accrual calculation","Whether leave days within a request are counted as working days or calendar days","Carryover behavior when approved leave spans the year boundary","Exact boundary semantics of contract end date (inclusive vs exclusive)","Whether medical leave for future dates follows standard or relaxed rules"],"assumptions":["'Month worked' means a full calendar month for accrual purposes","The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days","Balance is checked both at submission time and at approval time","'Working days' for advance notice excludes weekends and company holidays","Forfeiture at year-end is an automated batch process on January 1"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial-month accrual is undefined. When an employee starts or ends employment mid-month, should accrual be pro-rated (e.g., 15 days worked in a 30-day month = 0.75 days) or only granted for fully completed months?","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR / People Operations","Payroll"],"options":["Pro-rate accrual based on days worked in the month","Only accrue for fully completed calendar months","Accrue for any month in which the employee worked at least 1 day"],"recommendation":"Pro-rate accrual based on working days in the month. This is the most equitable approach and avoids penalizing employees who start mid-month."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Leave request day counting method is unspecified. When an employee requests leave from Monday to Friday, does a weekend in the middle consume 5 days or 7 days from their balance?","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR / People Operations"],"options":["Count only working days within the leave range","Count all calendar days within the leave range"],"recommendation":"Count only working days. This is standard practice in most HR systems and aligns with the spec's use of 'working days' for the notice period."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Year-end carryover interaction with approved leave spanning the year boundary is undefined. If an employee has 14 days balance and 5 approved leave days spanning Dec 29 – Jan 4, is carryover calculated on 14 (gross) or 9 (net of approved)?","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Finance","HR / People Operations"],"options":["Carryover on gross balance (14 days) — deduct leave as it is taken in January","Carryover on net balance (9 days) — treat approved leave as already consumed"],"recommendation":"Carryover on net balance (deduct approved leave before applying cap). This prevents a scenario where carryover forfeits days the employee has already been approved to take."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Contract end date boundary semantics are unclear. The spec says leave cannot span 'across' the end date, but does not clarify if the end date itself is a valid leave day (last day of employment) or the first day of non-employment.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR / People Operations"],"options":["Contract end date is the last day of employment — leave on that date is allowed","Contract end date is the first day of non-employment — leave up to but not including that date is allowed"],"recommendation":"Clarify with HR. Convention in most systems is that the contract end date is the last day of employment, meaning leave on that date should be permitted but not after."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
